# sweb_monitoring_contact (Resource)

Manages one monitoring contact — an email, phone, or Telegram notification destination (addEmail/addPhone/addTelegram + editContact/deleteContact on /monitoring/contacts). Create returns the contact `id` directly, which is the resource identity. `type` and `value` force replacement; `name` updates in place. `verified` is read-only (Telegram requires an out-of-band bot verification).

## Example Usage

```terraform
# A monitoring notification contact. type and value force replacement; name
# updates in place. type is one of email, phone, telegram.
resource "sweb_monitoring_contact" "ops" {
  type  = "email"
  value = "ops@example.com"
  name  = "Ops mailbox"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Human-readable label for the contact. Updated in place via the type's edit method.
- `type` (String) Contact type: one of email, phone, telegram. Forces replacement.

### Optional

- `value` (String) The contact address — the email or phone number. Omitted for telegram (which carries no value). Forces replacement.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Resource id (the numeric contact id assigned by the API).
- `verified` (Boolean) Whether the contact is confirmed and able to receive notifications (read-only).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import an existing contact by its numeric id (as shown by `sweb monitoring contact list`).
terraform import sweb_monitoring_contact.ops 678
```
